FT_IC1.evidence: Analyzes U.S. ABCD Study baseline data from 11,834 children ages 9-10 years

FT_IC5.evidence: Reports original empirical analysis: confirmatory factor analysis of bifactor model plus multiple linear regression and mixed-effects modeling

FT_IC2.evidence: Mixed-effects model pairs screen time activity (bundled with sleep, physical activity, income, gender) with p factor. Screen time is not isolated as a phone/SM-specific variable; composite includes physical activity and sleep with no separate breakout. Other side includes demographic (gender, household income) variables, not individual-trait-level

notes: Exclusion code FT-EC2: composite screen time bundled with non-SM variables (physical activity, sleep) without breakout; other-side demographic-only

FT_IC1.evidence: Describes U.S. ABCD Study infrastructure and data collection across 21 U.S. recruitment sites

FT_IC5.evidence: Commentary/perspective article describing the ABCD Study infrastructure, data availability, and future research opportunities; no original empirical analysis or statistical results reported

notes: Exclusion code FT-EC3: non-empirical publication (commentary article)

FT_IC1.evidence: Cross-sectional observational study using baseline data from ABCD Study with 10,712 children aged 9-10 years across 21 U.S. sites

FT_IC5.evidence: Reports original empirical analysis: latent profile analysis to identify family typologies followed by multilevel logistic regression examining associations with movement behavior recommendations

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed journal publication in International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1 search: no phone/SM-specific variable with own coefficient in results. Step 2: composite is 24-hour movement behaviors combining physical activity, screen time, and sleep; includes non-phone/non-SM activities (physical activity, sleep) without separate breakout for screen time. Other side is family functioning (parental acceptance, monitoring, conflict) — family environment characteristics are not individual-trait-level variables

notes: Exclusion code FT-EC2: composite 24-h movement behaviors bundles screen time with physical activity and sleep without separate breakout; other-side family environment variables do not qualify as individual-trait-level
